query: Draft a safe, concise radio check-in script for a SAR field team starting a shift. Keep it professional and clear.
You’re the planning chief. Create a 6-item checklist for a UAS (drone) pre-flight before a grid search over mixed terrain.
Extract any latitude/longitude coordinates from this: 'Team Alpha last reported at 37.7932 N, 122.3940 W near Pier 14.' Return JSON with keys lat and lon.
Given wind NW 15 kt, light rain, temps 48–52°F, draft a short safety brief for ground teams operating near coastal cliffs.
Make a simple ICS-style tasking note: assignment for Team Bravo to sweep Sectors C1–C3 on foot for 2 hours, check culverts, report findings by radio.
Summarize hazards from this NWS note in one paragraph: Strong onshore winds 15–25 mph with gusts to 35 this afternoon. Brief heavy showers. Small hail possible in isolated cells.
context: NWS: Strong onshore winds 15–25 mph with gusts to 35 this afternoon. Brief heavy showers. Small hail possible in isolated cells.
